news.title: Analysts Review Arm Holdings (ARM) Revenue Projections
news.summary: Arm Holdings plc (NASDAQ:ARM) is one of the 10 best NASDAQ growth stocks to buy for the next 10 years. news.title: Arm Holdings Expects Slower Revenue Growth in Fourth-Quarter
news.summary: The British semiconductor company forecast slower revenue growth despite rising demand for its chip designs for artificial intelligence data centers.

news.title: Arm Holdings Third-Quarter Profit Falls Despite Revenue Growth
news.summary: The British semiconductor company logged lower profit in its latest quarter despite a rise in sales due to increased demand for its chips in artificial intelligence data centers.

news.title: Arm Holdings shares fall as licensing sales miss estimates
news.summary: Shares of Arm Holdings fell on Wednesday as its licensing revenues slightly missed Wall Street ​estimates, despite a push by the company to boost the segment with ‌new chip technology offerings.  Shares were down 6% in after-hours trading after Arm reported results.  It also forecast fourth-quarter revenue ‌above Wall Street estimates on Wednesday, driven by demand for its energy-efficient chip designs used in artificial intelligence applications from data centers to smartphones.

news.title: ARM Holdings Q3 Adj. EPS $0.43 Beats $0.41 Estimate, Sales $1.242B Beat $1.223B Estimate
news.summary: ARM Holdings (NASDAQ:ARM) reported quarterly earnings of $0.43 per share which beat the analyst consensus estimate of $0.41 by 5.65 percent. This is a 10.26 percent increase over earnings of $0.39 per share from the same

news.title: Arm Holdings Looks for 9th Consecutive Earnings Beat Today
news.summary: Arm Holdings reports FY2026 Q3 earnings today, Feb. 4, after market close. Wall Street expects earnings of 41 cents per share, representing a 5.1% year-over-year (YOY) increase, on revenue of $1.23 billion, representing 25.7% YOY growth. news.title: Arm Holdings (NasdaqGS:ARM) Valuation Check After Recent Share Price Weakness
news.summary: What Arm Holdings stock performance looks like right now Arm Holdings (NasdaqGS:ARM) has recently seen weaker share performance. The stock is down about 2% over the past day, 9% over the past week, and roughly 35% over the past 3 months. Against that backdrop, investors are paying closer attention to the company’s current fundamentals, including reported revenue of US$4.4b and net income of US$830m.
